resources.title: Available Resources
resources.description: See the models you can call, then start using them in your client with your own API key.
resources.gettingStarted.title: Start using the campus shared API
resources.gettingStarted.use.title: Use the shared API
resources.gettingStarted.use.description: You do not need to configure or donate a channel to use the shared service. Create your own API key, then enter the Base URL and model name shown on the key page in Chatbox, Cherry Studio, or another OpenAI-compatible client.
resources.gettingStarted.use.step2: Copy the Base URL and key
resources.gettingStarted.donate.title: Want to support classmates?
resources.gettingStarted.donate.descript: If you have an upstream API, subscription, or Coding Plan to share, you can donate a channel. It is never required to use the shared service.
resources.privacyNotice: Only attribution metadata such as channel name, provider, description, and contributor is public here. Connection URLs and credentials remain hidden. Contributors can adjust only the simple model capabilities of their own channels.
resources.models.title: Models you can call
resources.models.description: This list reflects the effective access of your own enabled API keys. Use the exact model names shown below when making requests.
resources.models.copyHint: Select a model name to copy it exactly
resources.models.emptyNoApiKeys: You do not have an enabled API key yet, so no models are currently callable.
resources.models.emptyForApiKey: This API key does not currently have access to any models.
resources.models.emptySearch: No model names match your search.
resources.donations.title: Channels supported by classmates
resources.donations.description: Every card names its contributor. Thank you to everyone who shares a resource with the campus community.
resources.manage.title: My channel model settings
resources.manage.description: Models are detected and configured automatically. If needed, you can adjust the simple capabilities of channels you contributed; project owners do not need to maintain them for you. These are capability declarations; actual behavior depends on the channel response.
resources.manage.empty: You have not contributed any channels that need model settings.
resources.manage.loadError: Your channel model settings could not be loaded.
resources.manage.dialogDescription: Automatic settings require no work. Create an override only when this channel needs different capabilities. This declares capabilities and does not force the upstream service to honor a token limit.
resources.manage.invalidContext: Enter a whole number from 1 to 10,000,000.
resources.manage.invalidOutput: Enter a positive whole number no greater than the context length, or leave this blank.
resources.manage.updateError: Model settings could not be updated. Please try again.
resources.channels.projectDescription: These resources are maintained by the project. Connection details and credentials are never public.
resources.channels.empty: There are no shared channels to display yet.
resources.channels.noProjectChannels: There are no project-maintained shared channels to display yet.
resources.error.title: Unable to load resources
resources.error.description: The resource catalog is temporarily unavailable. Please try again shortly.
